svg.title: Frontier serving DAG
svg.desc: Frontier serving DAG: dag diagram, 7 nodes, 8 edges. DAG · one key routes to three frontier labs, every lab shares the cache, telemetry skips the ranks to metrics. Flow: requests → OpenRouter; OpenRouter → Anthropic (route); OpenRouter → OpenAI; OpenRouter → Gemini; Anthropic → cache (cache); OpenAI → cache; …. Full data in hw:payload.

hw:reasoning.hw:intent: Topology graph on a transparent ground — nodes as 1px cards (rx12, hero rx14 at 1.5px in the signal accent), flow carried by dashed connectors and the closed motion grammar; reads as the same hand that drew the badges and the matrix, not a generic graphviz dump.
hw:reasoning.hw:approach: Every layout is a closed-form solve from chassis constants: the pipeline distributes card widths over a 896px banner with 60px gaps; fans share midpoint S-curves (control points at the chord's x midpoint); the flywheel trims its 178px arcs by each card's angular half-width plus 1.2 degrees (the specimen's 24-degree inset at any ring count). Connectors run a marching 5-7 dash (1.618s) with particle riders — compositor-only properties; geometry never moves. Radial spokes draw under the hub card so they emanate from its surface.
hw:reasoning.hw:tradeoffs: Direction is carried by motion, never arrowheads — a reduced-motion or rasterized embed falls back to dashed tubes and reading order alone. Beam/flow repaint per frame, so they declare performance=paint-ok and degrade to particle/dash on composite-only surfaces; dark papers run brighter 400-level flow hues, trading cross-variant hue identity for legible strokes on near-black grounds.
